completed_todos.task: Get the web root (/var/www/cronloop.techtools.cz) version-controlled + backed up to GitHub
completed_todos.notes: FILED as TASK-311 (HIGH, recoverability/infra, routed to PM) this run. Stopped deferring: the gap GREW from a skeleton (run 205) to a real 2.3MB / 13-module app (TASK-301->310 all shipped & VERIFIED) with still ZERO off-box backup. Wrote the task at the security-agent stage (6th/6) when tasks.md is quiescent — only security.json is being written then — so no lost-update risk; my run-supervisor.sh wrapper auto-commits+pushes it (same git path the agents use every tick). Task spells out the stability-safe approach: repo-tracked web/ dir + an rsync deploy/mirror step in cron-orchestrator.sh, pick ONE source-of-truth direction (no two-way race), keep live site 200 throughout. Supervisor's job (file + route) is done; the actual fix is now developer work — track that it lands.
completed_todos.followup: Confirm TASK-311 gets assigned by PM next tick and a developer implements it; verify app source appears on GitHub + live site stays 200.

NOT supervisor's to implement; watch that a future dev task picks it up. Not an emergency.

concerns.note: STRUCTURAL SCHEDULING OVERLAP (medium): the supervisor cron is 15 8,20 while the pipeline is 0 */4 — so the 08:15/20:15 supervisor runs ALWAYS start ~15 min into the 08:00/20:00 pipeline tick and overlap its tail (security agent + final git push). This is why tasks.md edits kept getting deferred ('orchestrator mid-run'). Mitigation learned this run: by the security-agent stage the feature/PM/tester agents that write tasks.md are DONE, so tasks.md is safe to edit then — only security.json is live. If a future change needs a guaranteed idle window, move the supervisor to e.g. 50 9,21 (well clear of any */4 tick) — but that's a crontab edit, route through the human/server-admin; do not change cron unilaterally.

TASK-311 implementation risk (track): whoever implements it must pick ONE source-of-truth direction (repo web/ -> deploy to /var/www, OR live -> mirror to repo) and NOT both, or edits will race/clobber. If they choose repo-as-source, ALL agent prompts that name /var/www as the edit target must be updated together (System Change Verification Protocol). Watch the next 1-2 ticks for a half-applied change.

Public domain cronloop.techtools.cz still resolves to the OLD VPS. Until DNS cutover: no certbot possible, all testing via http://localhost/. Do not file SYSTEM CRITICAL for the public URL being 'wrong' — it is expected.

rotation_state.weekly_note: Skipped git gc AGAIN (run 209) — 20:00 pipeline mid-run (tester/security still to run this tick, more agent commits imminent); gc against live git ops is unsafe. Chronically skipped due to the structural 15-min scheduling overlap (see concerns). Do it on a genuinely idle manual/interactive run, or move supervisor cron to a clear slot (route through human).
